prompt:
Post-mortem action item from last week: the nightly maintenance runner behaved differently on two hosts and we spent an hour figuring out that one wrapper passed `-Purge:$false` while the other just omitted `-Purge`. Same effective value, totally different intent, and nothing in the logs to tell them apart.

I want a tiny audit shim, `flagcard.ps1` in the repo root, that takes EXACTLY the runner's five switches and prints a card saying what was actually on the command line. The five, all `[switch]` parameters: `-Archive`, `-DryRun`, `-FollowSymlinks`, `-Purge`, `-Rescan`. All of the colon forms must work (`-Purge`, `-Purge:$true`, `-Purge:$false`) — that's standard switch binding, don't reinvent it.

The card, on stdout, LF line endings: one line per flag in ordinal name order, shaped

flag <Name> value=<True|False> int=<0|1> source=<explicit|default>

where `value` is the switch as a bool, `int` is the same thing coerced to 0/1 (our metrics side wants the number), and `source` is `explicit` if the flag appeared on the command line IN ANY FORM — including `-Purge:$false` — and `default` if it didn't. That distinction is the entire point of the tool; `$PSBoundParameters` knows, use it. After the five flag lines:

bound: <comma-joined ordinal-sorted names of the flags that were explicitly passed, or (none)>
explicit: <count> of 5

Anything else on the command line — a stray word, an unknown `-Whatever` — should die with PowerShell's own binding error (exit 1, nothing on stdout). Don't catch it, don't wrap it; the shim must never guess. Success is exit 0 with nothing on stderr.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_flagcard.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P1 item 3: [switch] semantics incl. -Flag:$false explicit negation + $PSBoundParameters introspection; the coercion table is the value=/int= columns. Probed 2026-07-16 on pwsh 7.6.3: the literal argument string '-Archive:$false' passed through -File binds the switch to False with $PSBoundParameters.ContainsKey('Archive') True (works both from bash single-quoted and from a pwsh harness via & pwsh -File @args); explicit-false vs absent is indistinguishable by value, only by boundness — that is the tested lesson and it has no bash/getopts analog. Stray positional and unknown-flag cases pin engine binding errors (exit 1, 'positional parameter cannot be found' / "parameter name 'Wipe'"). No domain overlap: no existing seed does CLI flag introspection (go-flagbind/go-flagengine are Go flag-parsing libraries, different layer and language).
